message:
feat: Extend conflict detection, pin FrankenRedis git deps, and enforce runtime abstraction

Addresses three interrelated improvements to operational safety and
determinism under rch worker and asupersync migration:

1. FrankenRedis dependency migration (ft-3681t.7.3.1.2):
   - Replace sibling path dependencies (../../../frankenredis/...) with
     pinned git dependencies at rev 7b00450d to fix rch worker topology
     drift — remote workers no longer require local frankenredis checkout
   - Add .cargo/config.toml.example with local path overrides for
     development patching
   - Update Cargo.lock with new git sources for fr-store, fr-protocol,
     fr-config, fr-expire, fr-persist

2. Extend mission_loop conflict detection for resource-aware orchestration:
   - Add KnownResourceReservation struct for tracking logical resource
     reservations (panes, sockets, locks)
   - Add ResourceReservationOverlap variant to ConflictType enum
   - Implement detect_resource_reservation_overlaps() with wildcard scope
     matching via resource_scopes_overlap() helper
   - New detect_conflicts_with_resources() public API wraps existing
     detect_conflicts() for backward compatibility (empty resource list)
   - Reorder detection phases: Phase 2 = resource reservations, Phase 3 =
     bead claims; include resource conflicts in high-priority messaging

3. Enforce runtime abstraction for test utilities (asupersync track):
   - Add start_paused() method to RuntimeBuilder (test-only, no-op for
     asupersync, forwards to tokio for tokio builds)
   - Migrate wezterm.rs from direct tokio::runtime::Builder to
     runtime_compat::RuntimeBuilder
   - Add tokio::runtime::Builder to forbidden-direct-import surface guard
   - Assert start_paused() availability in runtime_compat contract tests

All changes are non-breaking and backward compatible.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","title":"[ops][rch] Fix remote-exec fallback when rch sync succeeds but remote exec fails and falls open local","description":"Observed in /Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm during compile-unblock work: rch selects workers and completes sync, then remote command execution fails with 'Failed to connect to ubuntu@<host>' and falls back to local execution ([RCH] local). Repro: rch exec -- cargo check --help. In contrast, manual ssh and rch workers capabilities --refresh succeed for all workers. This blocks policy-compliant offloaded cargo validation for active beads. Goal: identify and fix exec-path connectivity mismatch, restore reliable remote execution, and capture validation evidence.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"OliveCreek","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:30:28.539385Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T01:56:33.429688Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","infrastructure","ops","rch"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.7.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:30:28.539385Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1847,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","author":"ChartreuseFox","text":"Taking non-overlap slice in-repo (scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh + tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh). Reproduced fail-open path: rch check/status healthy, workers probe all RCH-E100, rch exec syncs then logs 'Remote execution failed ... running locally' and emits '[RCH] local'. Implementing stricter validator + e2e guard for remote-exec failure signature.","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:39:44Z"},{"id":1848,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Completed non-overlap guardrail slice:
- scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh: heavy runs now require fallback metadata when worker_context indicates local/fallback even if used_rch=true (captures rch fail-open cases).
- scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh self-test extended with fail-open negative/positive coverage.
- tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh: added RCH fail-open regex guard ("[RCH] local", "Remote execution failed: ... running locally", "Failed to connect to ubuntu@").
- tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh: added preflight rch remote smoke step (rch exec -- env TMPDIR=/tmp cargo --version) and hard-fail on fail-open signature before heavy test/check steps.
Validation:
- bash scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh --self-test  => pass
- bash -n scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh => pass
- bash tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh => exits 2 with 'no reachable rch workers; refusing local fallback' (expected current infra state)
Artifacts:
- tests/e2e/logs/ft_124z4_20260304_024259.jsonl
- tests/e2e/logs/ft_124z4_20260304_024259.stdout.log","created_at":"2026-03-04T02:43:36Z"},{"id":1981,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"DarkCliff assist 2026-03-07: added a local-side macOS guardrail for the rch fail-open bug in this repo's existing policy harness.

Findings:
- Reproduced a distinct local-side root cause not covered by the earlier remote-failure-only guard: when local TMPDIR is a long /var/folders/... path on macOS, rch's SSH ControlMaster socket path exceeds the Unix socket limit.
- Symptom: workers probe / remote exec can fail even though manual SSH works; rch then risks fail-open local execution.
- Confirmed workaround: prefix local rch invocations with TMPDIR=/tmp. Setting TMPDIR only inside the remote command is insufficient because the failure happens in the local rch SSH wrapper.

Changes:
- tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh now routes every rch invocation through a Darwin-aware wrapper that applies TMPDIR=/tmp locally, logs that workaround, and broadens fail-open detection to include the socket-path-too-long signature.
- scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh self-test now covers env-prefixed rch commands (TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- ...) so policy classification continues to treat them as compliant rch usage.

Validation:
- bash -n scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh ✅
- bash scripts/validate_asupersync_rch_execution_policy.sh --self-test ✅
","created_at":"2026-03-07T20:49:27Z"},{"id":1983,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"DarkCliff follow-up 2026-03-07: corrected the preflight smoke in tests/e2e/test_ft_124z4.sh from `rch exec -- cargo --version` to `rch exec -- cargo check --help`.

Why:
- `cargo --version` is classified by rch as a non-compilation command and can run locally, so it does not prove remote exec.
- `cargo check --help` stays lightweight but exercises the actual remote compilation execution path.

Live verification:
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- cargo check --help` selected worker `vmi1149989`, synced the project, executed remotely, and completed with `[RCH] remote vmi1149989`.
","created_at":"2026-03-07T20:51:31Z"},{"id":2037,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.7.3.1","author":"WhiteFox","text":"Additional `rch` failure mode reproduced while validating `ft-3681t.7.3.2`.

Evidence:
- `TMPDIR=/tmp rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_whitefox_check cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --message-format short` succeeded remotely on `vmi1149989` (`[RCH] remote vmi1149989`).
- Repeated targeted remote test commands (`cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib command_uses_rch` and `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib robot_heavy_cargo_without_rch_requires_approval`) were scheduled to `vmi1227854` and failed immediately with remote exit 101 before compilation.
- Failure signature on `vmi1227854`:
  `failed to read /home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankenredis/crates/fr-config/Cargo.toml`

Interpretation:
- This looks like worker-topology drift / inconsistent sibling-repo provisioning across the fleet: some workers (at least `vmi1149989`) have the required `frankenredis` sibling tree, while others (`vmi1227854`) do not.
- This is distinct from the macOS TMPDIR/SSH-control-socket workaround: even with `TMPDIR=/tmp`, remote execution can still fail if the selected worker lacks the expected sibling repo layout.

Potential follow-up:
- audit which workers have `/home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankenredis/...` populated
- make worker topology consistent or teach `rch` selection to avoid workers missing required sibling repos for this project
- revisit `.rch/config.toml` exclusions and remote path expectations so path dependencies are not silently worker-dependent.
","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:56:33Z"}]}

-{"id":"ft-e34d9","title":"Epic: Replace tokio async runtime with asupersync","description":"# Epic: Replace tokio async runtime with asupersync

## Background
FrankenTerm currently uses tokio 1.49.0 (full features) as its async runtime, with ~1,090 async patterns across 61 files. The asupersync library (https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/asupersync) is a spec-first, cancel-correct, capability-secure Rust async runtime that replaces tokio entirely.

## Why asupersync?
1. **Structured concurrency** - Every spawned task belongs to a Scope; no orphan tasks possible
2. **Cancel-correctness** - Multi-phase cancellation protocol (Request→Drain→Finalize) vs tokio's silent drop
3. **Capability security** - All effects gated through Cx context token; no ambient authority
4. **Four-valued Outcome<T,E>** - Ok/Err/Cancelled/Panicked vs Result's Ok/Err
5. **Two-phase channel ops** - Reserve/Commit pattern eliminates "message lost on cancellation" bugs
6. **Deterministic testing** - LabRuntime with virtual time, seed-controlled scheduling, DPOR exploration
7. **Built-in networking** - TCP/UDP/TLS/HTTP/gRPC without tokio transitive deps

+{"id":"ft-e34d9","title":"Epic: Replace tokio async runtime with asupersync","description":"# Epic: Replace tokio async runtime with asupersync

## Background
FrankenTerm currently uses tokio 1.49.0 (full features) as its async runtime, with ~1,090 async patterns across 61 files. The asupersync library (https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/asupersync) is a spec-first, cancel-correct, capability-secure Rust async runtime that replaces tokio entirely.

## Why asupersync?
1. **Structured concurrency** - Every spawned task belongs to a Scope; no orphan tasks possible
2. **Cancel-correctness** - Multi-phase cancellation protocol (Request→Drain→Finalize) vs tokio's silent drop
3. **Capability security** - All effects gated through Cx context token; no ambient authority
4. **Four-valued Outcome<T,E>** - Ok/Err/Cancelled/Panicked vs Result's Ok/Err
5. **Two-phase channel ops** - Reserve/Commit pattern eliminates "message lost on cancellation" bugs
6. **Deterministic testing** - LabRuntime with virtual time, seed-controlled scheduling, DPOR exploration
7. **Built-in networking** - TCP/UDP/TLS/HTTP/gRPC without tokio transitive deps

## Scope
Replace ALL tokio usage across the codebase:
- Core runtime and task spawning → asupersync Scope/Cx
- Sync primitives (Mutex, RwLock, Semaphore) → asupersync sync
- Channels (mpsc, watch, broadcast) → asupersync channels
- Network I/O (UnixStream, TcpListener) → asupersync net
- Time (sleep, timeout) → asupersync via Cx
- select! macro → asupersync combinators
- tokio-rustls → asupersync TLS
- reqwest → asupersync HTTP client
- Test infrastructure → LabRuntime

 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10","title":"[asupersync][deep] Program: profound runtime integration and harmonization","description":"Program umbrella for making /dp/asupersync the coherent runtime substrate across FrankenTerm core + vendored crates.

This program is explicitly about deep harmonization, not piecemeal API replacement:
- shared concurrency doctrine (Scope/Cx/Outcome/cancellation checkpoints)
- runtime behavior consistency across CLI/robot/watch/web/mux paths
- deterministic validation, observability, and strict dependency hygiene
- operationally safe cutover with rollback evidence

Goal: eliminate semantic drift between old async assumptions and asupersync’s correctness model so the system behaves predictably under load, failures, and cancellation.

## Success Criteria
1. The complete epic scope is delivered across child beads with no unresolved critical blockers and no loss of declared capability.
2. Every child feature/task in this epic includes comprehensive unit tests plus deterministic integration/E2E scenarios.
3. The epic includes an end-to-end validation matrix covering happy paths, failure injection, recovery behavior, and sustained/soak execution where applicable.
4. Structured logging and artifact standards are enforced across epic deliverables (timestamp, correlation_id, scenario_id, decision path, reason_code/error_code) with triage-ready evidence bundles.
5. Verification demonstrates stable, repeatable outcomes across reruns and confirms policy/safety invariants for all touched subsystems.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Produce and execute a complete dependency-ordered plan that migrates runtime semantics (not just imports) to asupersync.\n2. Program closure requires zero untracked runtime islands and explicit proof of operational parity/improvement.\n4. Program closure requires audited evidence bundle: benchmarks, failure-injection outcomes, rollback rehearsal artifacts, and cutover sign-off records.\n5. All heavy compile/test/benchmark workloads are executed via rch exec -- <command> with command evidence recorded.","notes":"This bead exists to prevent fragmented migration.
